title: Make the chat model provider configurable via environment variables
description:
Repository: https://github.com/MuhammedSenn/novella_ai
Branch: main

Requirement:
In src/graph.py the chat model is hardcoded to Google Gemini. Add a get_llm(provider, model) factory function in src/graph.py that returns the matching LangChain chat model: 'google' -> ChatGoogleGenerativeAI, 'openai' -> ChatOpenAI, 'anthropic' -> ChatAnthropic, 'ollama' -> ChatOllama. Read provider and model from the LLM_PROVIDER and LLM_MODEL environment variables using os.getenv, defaulting to provider 'google' and model 'gemini-2.5-flash' so current behavior is unchanged when the variables are unset. Raise ValueError for an unknown provider. Use lazy imports inside the factory so importing graph.py does not require every provider SDK to be installed. Do NOT modify .env or pyproject.toml, and do NOT fix unrelated pre-existing bugs in other files.

Acceptance Criteria:
- get_llm with provider 'google' returns a ChatGoogleGenerativeAI instance
- get_llm with an unknown provider raises ValueError
- When LLM_PROVIDER is unset, the default provider is google with model gemini-2.5-flash (behavior unchanged)
- Provider SDK imports are lazy (inside get_llm) so importing the module does not require the openai/anthropic/ollama packages
- Add unit tests under tests/ that mock the provider classes (do not import real provider SDKs) covering the default provider, an explicitly selected provider, and the unknown-provider ValueError
